query: html form default method interview question

competitors.label: <form>: The Form element
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical MDN reference for the form element, including the method attribute, GET defaults, and how browsers submit form data.
theirStrengths: MDN is authoritative on form submission semantics, GET and POST behavior, and the baseline structure of the form element.
theirGaps: The reference format is less explicit about the production security mistake of accidentally leaking sensitive data via the default GET method.

realWorldUseCases: HTML forms default to GET, which puts form fields into the URL query string. That is useful for search and filtering, but a production security pitfall when teams accidentally send private data without switching to POST.
followUpConfusion: If a form omits the method attribute, the browser defaults to GET. That means fields are serialized into the URL query string.

realWorldUseCases: MDN explains form submission semantics well, but it is lighter on the exact production bug this question usually targets: sensitive data ending up in URLs, logs, and history.
actionableExamples: It includes canonical form examples and method notes, so the example criterion stays competitive.
followUpConfusion: Our page is more direct on the follow-up confusion between search-friendly GET flows and sensitive workflows that should move to POST.

actionableExamples: Keep the GET-versus-POST comparison visible so the answer stays practical, not only definitional.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical form reference, but our page is stronger as an interview answer because it makes the default-GET rule concrete through search use cases, security leakage, and debugging consequences.
